content:
# Session 4 (iter-004) ‚ÄĒ review summary

## Metadata
- Iter/session: 004 / session_4. Mode: `prove`. 2 lanes: M1 `WeilDivisor.lean`, S1 `OcOfD.lean` (both mathlib-build, both gate-cleared this iter via the same-iter fast path).
- Sorry count: WeilDivisor 3‚Üí2; OcOfD 4‚Üí4 (sharpened, see below). All touched files build green, all new decls axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
- sync_leanok (iter-004): added 0 / removed 0. blueprint-doctor: **no structural findings**.

## Targets

### M1 ‚ÄĒ `finite_order_support_affine` (WeilDivisor.lean L892) ‚ÄĒ SOLVED ‚úď (axiom-clean)
The recurring CHURNING√ó2 blocker (Hartshorne II.6.1 affine core) is **CLOSED**.
- Proof = the full (i)(ii)(iii) AG scheme‚Üíring bridge. `R := őď(V,‚ä§)` Noetherian domain with `IsFractionRing R K(V)`; support `{Z | ord_Z g ‚Č† 0}` injected into the finite set of height-1 primes containing the numerator/denominator of `g=a/b` (`Ideal.finite_setOf_isPrime_height_one_mem_or`, iter-003) via `Z ‚Ü¶ (hV.primeIdealOf ‚ü®Z.point,_‚ü©).asIdeal`; closed by `Set.Finite.of_finite_image`.
- **The unlock (KB):** earlier iters assumed an explicit `ūĚí™_{V,Z} ‚ČÖ Localization.AtPrime` ring-iso + `ordFrac_ringEquiv` transport. NOT needed ‚ÄĒ `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk` gives the stalk as an abstract `IsLocalization.AtPrime` directly, so `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` applies with no transport. The previous "authorized-gap" escalation is **retired**.
- Minor build hiccup: `push_neg at hcon` deprecation ‚Üí replaced by `rw [not_or] at hcon`; injectivity `rwa [hV.fromSpec_primeIdealOf, ...]` ‚Üí `simpa only [hV.fromSpec_primeIdealOf] using hpt`.

### M1 aux ‚ÄĒ `Ring.ordFrac_eq_one_of_notMem` (WeilDivisor.lean L860) ‚ÄĒ SOLVED ‚úď (axiom-clean, NEW)
Step-(iii) ring core: for `IsLocalization.AtPrime A pp` with common fraction field `K`, `a,b ‚ąČ pp ‚üĻ ordFrac A (a/b) = 1`. 7-line proof: `IsLocalization.map_units` ‚Üí units ‚Üí `Ring.ordFrac_of_isUnit` ‚Üí `div_one`. Stated abstractly over `IsLocalization.AtPrime A pp` (not literal `Localization.AtPrime`) precisely so it lands on the stalk with no ring-iso transport.

### S1 ‚ÄĒ `isDiscreteValuationRing_stalk_of_finrank_cotangentSpace_eq_one` (OcOfD.lean L142) ‚ÄĒ SOLVED ‚úď (axiom-clean, NEW wiring lemma)
Scheme-stalk wrapper of Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_CotangentSpace_eq_one_iff`. For `X [IsIntegral][IsLocallyNoetherian]`, `x:X`: `finrank őļ(x) (CotangentSpace (stalk x)) = 1 ‚Üí IsDiscreteValuationRing (stalk x)`. One line `‚Ä¶_iff.mp hone`; the 3 stalk typeclass facts (`IsLocalRing`, `IsNoetherianRing` from `IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsDomain` from `IsIntegral`) resolve by inference. Packages the whole codim-1 DVR substrate so the only remaining input is the numeric `finrank = 1`.

### S1 ‚ÄĒ `sheafOf` substrate sorry (OcOfD.lean L635) ‚ÄĒ BLOCKED (RETARGETED, not closed)
The `D‚Č†0` branch's `IsRegularInCodimensionOne` witness now carries ONE typed sorry: `finrank őļ(Y.point) (CotangentSpace (stalk Y.point)) = 1`, routed to DVR through the new wiring lemma. This **bypasses `IsRegularLocalRing` entirely** (the iter-004 re-scope mandate; the general smooth‚üĻregular route Stacks 056S/00TT was also absent from Mathlib). Sorry count unchanged 4‚Üí4; value is qualitative ‚ÄĒ the gap is now sharp and blueprint-endorsed (`lem:cotangentSpace_finrank_eq_one_of_smooth`).
- **The residual gap** = conormal iso `m_x/m_x¬≤ ‚ČÖ ő©_{C/kŐĄ,x} ‚äó őļ(x)` at a rational point (Hartshorne II.8.7). A genuine **multi-theorem Mathlib gap**, not wiring. Exhaustive scouting (local_search, leansearch√ó6, loogle√ó6, leanfinder√ó3) confirms Mathlib ships **none** of the 3 ingredients: (1) ő© locally free of rank 1 for the *stalk* (Mathlib only has it for finite-presentation `IsStandardSmooth`, not the localization); (2) codim-1 pt is kŐĄ-rational; (3) the conormal iso itself. No regularity shortcut either (`IsRegularLocalRing`-from-smoothness, `FormallySmooth‚üĻregular` both loogle-empty). Informal agent unavailable (no LLM key in env).

## Subagent dispatches (all returned; reports in `logs/iter-004/`)
- **lean-auditor/iter004** (22 files): 0 must-fix code defects, **no laundered sorries**. Highest finding **F-1** = docstring/body mismatch on `isRegularInCodimOneProjectiveLineBar` (WeilDivisor ~L1677): body is a **fully closed axiom-clean proof** but docstring says "Typed-sorry theorem" ‚ÄĒ misleads future provers (route to prover/refactor; review can't edit `.lean`). F-2‚Ä¶F-5 stale status headers; F-6‚Ä¶F-8 out-of-cone GmScaling/BareScheme substrate sorries (well-documented, not laundered). Report: `task_results/lean-auditor-iter004.md`.
- **lvb-checker √ó 2** (both `0 must-fix`): `weildivisor` ‚ÄĒ `\leanok` on `lem:finite_order_support_affine` now **genuinely earned** (sorry-free, axiom-clean), proof faithful to sketch; 1 major = `Ring.ordFrac_eq_one_of_notMem` owes a block + `\uses` entry. `ocofd` ‚ÄĒ retargeted sorry matches the cotangent route ‚úď, new wiring lemma axiom-clean ‚úď; 2 major = (a) wiring lemma unpinned on live path, (b) 5 cotangent-route `\lean{}` pins dangle on decls absent from the whole project (the planned decomposition not yet implemented). Reports: `task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-{weildivisor,ocofd}.md`.

## Key findings / patterns
- **M1 closed by locating the right Mathlib infra, not by forcing a gap.** `isLocalization_stalk` + `functionField_isScalarTower` + `ordFrac_of_isUnit` collapse the AG bridge to mechanical plumbing; the assumed ring-iso-transport route was a red herring across 2 iters.
- **OcOfD is now a single named research gap** (conormal iso II.8.7), parallel in character to the existing `lem:serre_finiteness_mathlib_gap` ‚ąě-hole. Both new routes (general regular, cotangent) confirmed absent from pinned Mathlib `b80f227`.
- DAG: `gaps` = 1 (`lem:serre_finiteness_mathlib_gap`, S2 authorised). `unmatched` = 141 (139 out-of-cone parent substrate; **2 new in-cone owe blocks** ‚ÄĒ see recommendations).

## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
- **None.** sync_leanok added/removed 0 and lvb-checkers confirm all markers consistent (`lem:finite_order_support_affine` `\leanok` now genuinely backed). The two WeilDivisor "pin drift" items the lvb flagged (`functionField_isFractionRing_of_affine`, `structureSheaf_stalkIso`) are **NOT dangling** ‚ÄĒ both pins name real Mathlib decls and the blocks already carry `\mathlibok`; the proof merely uses a morally-equivalent sibling lemma. Cosmetic; left for the planner's blueprint-writer (owns prose/`\uses`). No `% NOTE:`/`\notready`/`\mathlibok`/`\lean{}` change was warranted on review side this iter.
